evaluations.evidence_quote: what we do is is we go through a very careful methodology where we go we steal man each hypothesis find the best explanation look for dep pendencies look for the best uh numbers give give a low range and a high range and uh and integrate all the numbers to something that is uh that is robust

I would say five to 10 times more so five to 10 times five so it's 25 to 50 cases... if you want I say five hospitalizations in addition to the five in the market but really you're you're you're trying to get something that I'm not claiming to have the entire model of the outbreak in my head

evaluations.evidence_quote: what we the data we brought about wi being in the center we explicitly said this is not something we believe in this is just showing you how easy it is to uh ch iic data we we're agreeing with you this that's sharpshoot a fallacy to claim that WBA supports wi the idea was to show how much you can play with the data
evaluations.reviewer_note: Acknowledges cherry-picking as a problem but simultaneously presents data that opponent identifies as suffering from selection bias (Weibo data, Texas Sharpshooter fallacy in their own analyses). evaluations.evidence_quote: you don't do factchecking and there's a bunch of cases where you messed up here you said that this December 8th case was happened before the market outbreak both sides of the debate disagree with that you you got the two visitors wrong... you talked about this Texas Sharpshooter fallacy... but then two slides later you're talking about Mr Chen on December 16th that's the same guy but you didn't notice that you've double labeled the same case
evaluations.reviewer_note: Opponent identifies multiple factual errors including misidentification of cases, incorrect dates, double-counting individuals, and mischaracterization of data. These errors indicate inadequate fact-checking prior to presentation.

Despite making extreme probabilistic claims (1 in 100,000), no documentation is provided of how these probabilities were calculated, which priors were used, how independence assumptions were validated, or how the multiplication of probabilities was justified.
